mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months over a 10-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of monetary tightening signals resilient supply-side growth that outpaces financing costs, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when industrial production growth exceeds 2% annually during tightening periods over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers market expectations of aggressive central bank policy tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of high-inflation trigger months across a 15-year historical backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm employment combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive monthly returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict return direction with accuracy above 52% in walk-forward validation over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue baselines and profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window, or quarterly returns show no statistical correlation with GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production amid elevated policy rates signal supply-side contraction and financial stress, causing negative forward S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS > 2.0%
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 10-year out-of-sample backtest.
